title: Florida Senate Bill Page: CS/CS/HJR 203, 2026 Session
description: Official bill page for CS/CS/HJR 203, including bill history, related bills, text, analyses, vote history, and last action.
reliability_notes: Primary official source for HJR 203 bill status and House floor vote.

title: Special Session D Memo and Information
description: Senate memo and planning materials for Special Session D.
reliability_notes: Official Senate planning source for Special Session D schedule and materials.

title: Update Regarding Special Session D Legislation
description: Official memo describing expected Special Session D legislation on redistricting, artificial intelligence, public records, and medical freedom.
reliability_notes: Official Senate source for the stated Special Session D legislative package as of April 24, 2026.

title: Florida Senate Bill Actions for April 28, 2026
description: Official Senate bill-actions page showing Special Session D bill actions for April 28, 2026.
reliability_notes: Primary official source for bills moving on the April 28, 2026 Senate bill-actions page.

title: Florida Senate Redistricting Page: Special Session 2026-D
description: Official Senate page describing congressional redistricting materials for Special Session 2026-D.
reliability_notes: Official Senate source for the redistricting purpose and materials of Special Session D.

title: Florida Senate Session Overview
description: Official Senate overview of regular sessions, special sessions, calendars, reports, and other legislative process materials.
reliability_notes: Official explanatory source for legislative session mechanics.

title: Florida Constitution: Article XI, Amendments
description: Official Florida Senate publication of Article XI of the Florida Constitution.
reliability_notes: Primary official source for constitutional amendment procedure.

title: Constitutional Amendments/Initiatives
publisher: Florida Department of State, Division of Elections
description: Department of State overview of proposed constitutional amendments and initiatives, including the 60 percent voter-approval requirement.
reliability_notes: Official election source for ballot-path explanation.
